claim: Skills allow you to bundle together scripts, templates, and instructions to define a specific capability for your AI
actionable: Create skill.md files in .github/skills folders with metadata, instructions, and references to scripts and templates to encapsulate reusable AI workflows.
source_title: The complete guide to Agent Skills

claim: Skills are progressively loaded into the context window, meaning only the name and description load initially, and full files load only when needed
actionable: Use skills to avoid bloating the initial prompt context; agents will read full skill files only when they invoke them, optimizing token usage.
source_title: The complete guide to Agent Skills

claim: Skills let you create modular reference documentation that agents can access for complex domains like PDF handling, brand guidelines, or API specs
actionable: Package domain knowledge into skill folders with comprehensive markdown documentation and reference assets so agents automatically load relevant context for specific tasks.
source_title: The complete guide to Agent Skills

claim: You should start a skill with required metadata: a name and description that are descriptive enough for the agent to pick them up automatically
actionable: Always include clear, specific name and description fields in skill.md metadata; vague descriptions prevent agents from knowing when to use the skill.
source_title: The complete guide to Agent Skills

claim: Use relative path syntax to reference scripts and templates within a skill folder
actionable: In skill.md files, reference scripts and templates using relative paths like ./scripts/get_system_info.js and ./templates/response.md
source_title: The complete guide to Agent Skills

claim: Skills are a spec from Anthropic that work across Claude Code, other IDEs, and non-editor tools
actionable: Recognize skills as a portable Anthropic standard; build them once and reuse across Claude Code, VS Code, Cursor, and other platforms.
source_title: The complete guide to Agent Skills
